text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Okonkwo, Renata
DOB: [on file]
SEX: Female
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [on file]
Referring Service: Surgical Oncology / Dermatologic Surgery

---

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:

A:  Skin, mid-back — excisional biopsy, primary lesion
B:  Right axillary sentinel lymph nodes — sentinel lymph node biopsy, 4 nodes submitted
C:  Subcutaneous abdominal wall nodule — core needle biopsy (concurrent)

---

PROCEDURE TYPE:
Specimen A: Wide local excision (excisional biopsy with 1 cm clinical margins), mid-back skin
Specimen B: Sentinel lymph node biopsy (right axillary basin), gamma probe-guided, 4 nodes retrieved
Specimen C: Core needle biopsy, subcutaneous abdominal wall nodule

---

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

A.  SKIN, MID-BACK, EXCISIONAL BIOPSY:
    - MALIGNANT MELANOMA, NODULAR TYPE, ARISING IN ASSOCIATION WITH A COMPOUND DYSPLASTIC NEVUS
    - Breslow thickness: 5.8 mm
    - Clark level: V (invasion into subcutaneous fat)
    - Ulceration: PRESENT
    - Mitotic rate: 8 mitoses per mm²
    - Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT
    - Perineural invasion: ABSENT
    - Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs): BRISK (diffuse)
    - Regression: ABSENT
    - Microsatellites: ABSENT
    - Satellite nodule: PRESENT (1 discrete nodule, 0.3 cm, within 2 cm of main tumor mass, separated by normal dermis)
    - Deep margin: POSITIVE (melanoma at inked deep margin)
    - Peripheral margins: NEGATIVE (closest peripheral margin 3 mm)
    - Background: Compound dysplastic nevus with moderate-to-severe architectural and cytologic atypia
    - pT stage (primary): pT4b (Breslow > 4 mm with ulceration)

B.  RIGHT AXILLARY SENTINEL LYMPH NODES, BIOPSY:
    - METASTATIC MALIGNANT MELANOMA in 3 of 4 sentinel lymph nodes
    - Largest metastatic deposit: 4.2 cm (gross involvement with extranodal extension)
    - Extranodal extension: PRESENT (largest node)
    - Remaining positive node deposits: 1.8 cm and 0.7 cm
    - One sentinel node: NEGATIVE for malignancy (0.8 cm reactive lymph node, no malignancy identified on H&E or IHC)
    - pN stage: pN3c (4 or more nodes involved OR any positive node with extranodal extension; here 3 of 4 positive with extranodal extension meeting pN3c criteria)

C.  SUBCUTANEOUS ABDOMINAL WALL NODULE, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
    - METASTATIC MALIGNANT MELANOMA
    - Morphology and immunophenotype identical to primary lesion (see IHC below)
    - pM stage: pM1c (distant soft tissue/subcutaneous metastasis)

---

COMBINED PATHOLOGIC STAGE (AJCC 8th Edition):
pT4b  pN3c  pM1c  —  Stage IV

---

GROSS PATHOLOGY DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A:  Received in formalin, labeled "mid-back skin excision." The specimen consists of an elliptical excision of skin and subcutaneous tissue measuring 5.5 x 3.8 cm in surface area and 2.4 cm in depth. On the dorsal surface, there is an irregularly pigmented, variably brown-to-black, raised lesion measuring 2.5 x 2.0 cm with irregular, ill-defined borders. A discrete satellite pigmented papule is noted approximately 1.7 cm from the main lesion, measuring 0.4 cm in greatest dimension. The cut surface reveals a dark-pigmented, lobulated, firm mass extending from the dermal-epidermal junction to the subcutaneous adipose tissue with an estimated gross depth of approximately 6 mm. No grossly definable plane is identified at the deep margin in the area of greatest depth. The peripheral margins are grossly uninvolved. The specimen is submitted entirely in cassettes A1–A12, with the deep margin inked black and peripheral margins inked blue.

Specimen B:  Received fresh in lymph node media, labeled "right axillary sentinel lymph nodes." Four lymph nodes are identified, measuring 4.2 cm, 1.8 cm, 0.7 cm, and 0.8 cm in greatest dimension. The largest node (4.2 cm) is largely replaced by a firm, pale-tan to focally pigmented mass with associated perinodal fatty infiltration consistent with extranodal extension grossly. The 1.8 cm and 0.7 cm nodes are partially replaced by similar tan-pigmented tissue. The 0.8 cm node appears grossly reactive without obvious tumor involvement. All nodes submitted entirely for histologic evaluation. Touch preparations performed intraoperatively (frozen section analysis was deferred to permanent sections given complexity).

Specimen C:  Received in formalin, labeled "subcutaneous abdominal wall nodule, core biopsy." Three cores of tan-white to focally pigmented tissue, ranging 1.0–1.4 cm in length and 0.1–0.2 cm in diameter. All cores submitted in entirety in cassette C1.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A (Primary Lesion):  Sections show a malignant melanocytic proliferation with a dominant nodular dermal component and an asymmetric, poorly circumscribed intraepidermal component. The tumor is composed of sheets and nests of large epithelioid melanocytes with marked nuclear pleomorphism, prominent eosinophilic macrolobulated nucleoli, and abundant pale to granular cytoplasm with intracytoplasmic melanin pigment. Multinucleated giant cells are present focally. The tumor extends through the full thickness of the dermis and invades the subcutaneous adipose tissue (Clark level V). Breslow thickness as measured from the granular layer to the deepest invasive focus measures 5.8 mm. Ulceration is present, involving approximately 30% of the surface area of the main nodule (ulcerated zone 0.7 cm in horizontal extent), with overlying fibrin and neutrophilic debris. Brisk tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes are present in a diffuse pattern throughout the base of the tumor. Mitoses are numerous, averaging 8 per mm² in the most active zones; atypical mitotic figures are present. Lymphovascular invasion is identified in dermal lymphatic channels at the base of the tumor. Perineural invasion is not identified on multiple levels. The adjacent epidermis and superficial dermis show residual compound dysplastic nevus with moderate-to-severe architectural atypia (bridging of rete ridges, shouldering) and cytologic atypia (nuclear enlargement, hyperchromasia) in the junctional component, consistent with the precursor lesion. The satellite nodule (specimen A) is confirmed on histology as a discrete melanocytic tumor deposit separated from the primary by uninvolved dermis. The deep margin is involved by tumor; peripheral margins are free with closest approach of 3 mm.

Specimen B (Sentinel Lymph Nodes):  Three of four nodes show metastatic melanoma. The largest node (4.2 cm) is almost entirely replaced by sheets of epithelioid melanocytes, with transgression of the nodal capsule and infiltration of perinodal adipose tissue (extranodal extension present, extent approximately 3 mm beyond capsule at the point of maximal extension). A second node (1.8 cm) shows a 1.2 cm confluent metastatic deposit in the subcapsular and medullary sinuses. A third node (0.7 cm) shows a 0.4 cm deposit. The fourth node (0.8 cm) shows reactive follicular hyperplasia and no evidence of malignancy on H&E or by immunohistochemistry.

Specimen C (Subcutaneous Abdominal Wall):  The cores show fibrous and adipose tissue infiltrated by cohesive nests and sheets of large epithelioid melanocytes with identical morphology to the primary lesion, including prominent nucleoli, intracytoplasmic melanin, and a high mitotic rate. No lymphoid tissue is identified. Findings are consistent with cutaneous/subcutaneous metastatic melanoma.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES — IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:

Performed on Specimens A, B, and C (paraffin blocks A4, B1, C1):

  SOX10:        POSITIVE (strong, diffuse nuclear staining in all specimens)
  S100 protein: POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic and nuclear staining in all specimens)
  HMB-45:       POSITIVE (strong, diffuse in all specimens)
  Melan-A (MART-1): POSITIVE (strong, diffuse in all specimens)
  MITF:         POSITIVE (nuclear, diffuse)
  Ki-67:        Approximately 45% labeling index (Specimen A, primary); approximately 48–50% in nodal metastases (Specimen B)
  PAN-CK (AE1/AE3): NEGATIVE (excludes carcinoma)
  CD45:         NEGATIVE in tumor cells (lymphoid cells in background positive, serving as internal control)
  Desmin:       NEGATIVE

  Note on BRAF IHC (VE1 clone, Specimen A): POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining consistent with BRAF V600E protein expression; reflexed to molecular testing per laboratory protocol)

Molecular / FISH studies: Pending at the time of this report — BRAF mutation analysis (molecular), and additional molecular profiling deferred to NGS platform per treating oncologist request.

Microsatellite Instability (by IHC panel — MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2): All four mismatch repair proteins show retained nuclear expression in tumor cells, consistent with microsatellite stability (MSS). (Note: Concordant with molecular MSS as reported on NGS.)

---

ADDITIONAL COMMENTS:

The overall morphologic and immunophenotypic findings are diagnostic of malignant melanoma, nodular type, with numerous adverse histologic features including a Breslow thickness of 5.8 mm, ulceration, Clark level V invasion, high mitotic rate, lymphovascular invasion, positive deep margin, satellite nodule formation, and extensive sentinel lymph node involvement with extranodal extension. The concurrent subcutaneous abdominal wall metastasis is confirmed. The combination of these findings is consistent with AJCC 8th edition Stage IV (pT4b pN3c pM1c) disease.

The brisk TIL pattern is noted and may be of prognostic and predictive significance in the context of immunotherapy.

The positive BRAF VE1 immunostain has been reflexed to PCR/Sanger-based BRAF mutation analysis for confirmation. Results to follow as an addendum.

DIAGNOSIS CONFIRMED BY: [Attending Pathologist, MD — Dermatopathology]
CASE REVIEWED BY: [Senior Staff Pathologist, MD]

--- END OF REPORT ---
